name: Entities
summary: The Entities class is a collection of Entity objects, either in a ComponentDefinition or directly in the Model. A Entities object corresponds to a drawing context in the GUI.

methods.summary: The #[] method is used to retrieve an entity by its index in an array of entities.
methods.remarks: The #[] method is used to retrieve an entity by its index in an array of entities. The index is a number between 0 and entities.length - 1. In general, it is preferable to use the #each method to iterate though all of the entities in the collection as it will be much more efficient.

params.name: entity_index
params.doc: The index for a specific entity.

returns.doc: an Sketchup::Entity object if successful, nil if not found

methods.name: add_3d_text
methods.signature: add_3d_text(string, alignment, font, is_bold = false, is_italic = false, letter_height = 1.0, tolerance = 0.0, z = 0.0, is_filled = true, extrusion = 0.0) => Boolean
methods.summary: The #add_3d_text method is used to create 3D text.
methods.remarks: The #add_3d_text method is used to create 3D text. It will be added as edges and faces drawn at the origin.

params.name: string
params.doc: The text to create.

params.name: alignment
params.doc: Number that defines the alignment. There are constants called TextAlignLeft, TextAlignRight, and TextAlignCenter that can be passed.

params.name: font
params.doc: font name.

params.name: is_bold
params.doc: true for bold.

params.name: is_italic
params.doc: true for italic.

params.name: letter_height
params.doc: Height of the text

params.name: tolerance
params.doc: Tolerance of the curve creation. Defaults to 0.0, which creates the highest possible curve quality.

params.name: z
params.doc: z position of the text

params.name: is_filled
params.doc: true for filled, which will put a face between the edges of the letters.

params.name: extrusion
params.doc: Extrusion depth

returns.doc: true if successful

methods.name: add_arc
methods.signature: add_arc(center, xaxis, normal, radius, start_angle, end_angle) => Array<Sketchup::Edge> | add_arc(center, xaxis, normal, radius, start_angle, end_angle, num_segments) => Array<Sketchup::Edge>
methods.summary: The add_arc method is used to create an arc curve segment.
methods.remarks: The add_arc method is used to create an arc curve segment.

params.name: center
params.doc: A Point3d object representing the center .

params.name: xaxis
params.doc: A Vector3d object representing xaxis for the arc.

params.name: normal
params.doc: A Vector3d object representing normal for the arc.

params.name: radius
params.doc: The radius of the arc.

params.name: start_angle
params.doc: Start angle for the arc, in radians.

params.name: end_angle
params.doc: End angle for the arc, in radians.

returns.doc: an array of Edge objects that define the arc.

methods.name: add_circle
methods.summary: The add_circle method is used to create a circle.
methods.remarks: The add_circle method is used to create a circle.

params.name: center
params.doc: A Point3d object representing the center.

params.name: normal
params.doc: A Vector3d object representing normal for the arc.

params.name: radius
params.doc: The radius of the arc.

params.name: numsegs
params.doc: The number of segments.

returns.doc: an Array object containing edges if successful

methods.name: add_cline
methods.signature: add_cline(start_point, end_point, stipple = '-') => Sketchup::ConstructionLine | add_cline(point, vector, stipple = '-') => Sketchup::ConstructionLine
methods.summary: The #add_cline method is used to create a construction line.
methods.remarks: The #add_cline method is used to create a construction line. This can be finite or infinite.

params.name: stipple
params.doc: See ConstructionLine#stipple= for acceptable patterns.

methods.name: add_cpoint
methods.summary: The add_cpoint method is used to create a construction point.
methods.remarks: The add_cpoint method is used to create a construction point.

params.name: point
params.doc: A Point3d object.

returns.doc: a ConstructionPoint object if successful

methods.name: add_curve
methods.summary: The add_curve method is used to create a curve from a collection of edges.
methods.remarks: The add_curve method is used to create a curve from a collection of edges. The arguments are either Points or an Array of Points. At least 2 points are required.

methods.name: add_dimension_linear
methods.signature: add_dimension_linear(start_pt_or_entity, end_pt_or_entity, offset_vector) => Sketchup::DimensionLinear | add_dimension_linear(start_instance_path, end_instance_path, offset_vector) => Sketchup::DimensionLinear | add_dimension_linear(start_array, end_array, offset_vector) => Sketchup::DimensionLinear
methods.summary: The #add_dimension_linear method adds a linear dimension to the entities.
methods.remarks: The #add_dimension_linear method adds a linear dimension to the entities.

params.name: start_pt_or_entity
params.doc: the reference point and/or entity at the start of the dimension. This parameter can take several forms:

params.name: end_pt_or_entity
params.doc: the reference point and/or entity at the end of the dimension. This parameter takes the exact same forms as 'start_pt_or_entity'.

params.name: offset_vector
params.doc: the parallel offset vector from the reference line to the dimension line measured from the 'start' reference point.

returns.doc: the created dimension

methods.name: add_dimension_radial
methods.summary: The add_dimension_radial method adds a radial dimension (i.
methods.remarks: The add_dimension_radial method adds a radial dimension (i.e arc/circle radius/diameter dimension) to the entities.

params.name: arc_curve
params.doc: an ArcCurve object to which the dimension is to be attached.

params.name: leader_break_pt
params.doc: a Point3d for the break point on the leader where the dimension text is attached.

returns.doc: the created dimension

methods.name: add_edges
methods.summary: Note: If the points form a closed loop, the first and last vertex will not merge.
methods.remarks: Note: If the points form a closed loop, the first and last vertex will not merge. If you intend to create a face from the edges, use #add_face directly. The #add_edges method is used to add a set of connected edges to the Sketchup::Entities collection.

params.name: points
params.doc: At least two points required.

methods.name: add_face
methods.summary: Note: A special case exists for any face created on the ground plane, in which case the vertex order is ignored and the face is always facing down.
methods.remarks: Note: A special case exists for any face created on the ground plane, in which case the vertex order is ignored and the face is always facing down. The add_face method is used to create a face. You can call this method a number of ways: For the last form that takes a Curve, the curve must be closed - like a circle.

methods.name: add_faces_from_mesh
methods.signature: add_faces_from_mesh(polygon_mesh, smooth_flags = Geom::PolygonMesh::AUTO_SOFTEN|Geom::PolygonMesh::SMOOTH_SOFT_EDGES, f_material = nil, b_material = nil) => Integer
methods.summary: The #add_faces_from_mesh method is used to add Face entities to the collection of entities from a Geom::PolygonMesh.
methods.remarks: The #add_faces_from_mesh method is used to add Face entities to the collection of entities from a Geom::PolygonMesh. The smooth_flags parameter can contain any of the following values if passed. The constants were added in SketchUp 2014. For previous versions, numeric values have to be specified instead of the Ruby constants. 0: Geom::PolygonMesh::NO_SMOOTH_OR_HIDE 1: Geom::PolygonMesh::HIDE_BASED_ON_INDEX (Negative point index will hide the edge.) 2: Geom::PolygonMesh::SOFTEN_BASED_ON_INDEX (Negative point index will soften the edge.) 4: Geom::PolygonMesh::AUTO_SOFTEN (Interior edges are softened.) 8: Geom::PolygonMesh::SMOOTH_SOFT_EDGES (All soft edges will also be smooth.) The 3rd and 4th parameters will accept a Material object or a string name of a material currently in the model.

params.name: smooth_flags
params.doc: flags for softening and smoothing of edges.

params.name: f_material
params.doc: material to paint front faces with.

params.name: b_material
params.doc: material to paint back faces with.

returns.doc: Number of faces created

methods.name: add_group
methods.summary: Note: Calling add_group with entities in its parameters has been known to crash SketchUp before version 8.
methods.remarks: Note: Calling add_group with entities in its parameters has been known to crash SketchUp before version 8.0. It is preferable to create an empty group and then add things to its Entities collection. The #add_group method is used to create a new group.

params.name: entities
params.doc: an entities collection to add to the group.

methods.name: add_image
methods.summary: The add_image method is used to add an image to the collection of entities.
methods.remarks: The add_image method is used to add an image to the collection of entities. The width and height are measured in model units (i.e. inches). If the height is not given, then it is computed from the width to preserve the aspect ratio of the image.

params.name: path
params.doc: A path for the image file.

params.name: point
params.doc: A Point3d object representing the insertion point of the image.

params.name: width
params.doc: Width for the image.

params.name: height
params.doc: height for the image if you want to control width and height independently. Leave as default 0.0 when you want it to be relative to the aspect ratio.

returns.doc: an Image object if successful.

methods.name: add_instance
methods.summary: The #add_instance method adds a group or component instance to the collection of entities using an existent definition.
methods.remarks: The #add_instance method adds a group or component instance to the collection of entities using an existent definition.

params.name: definition
params.doc: A ComponentDefinition object.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A Transformation object.

returns.doc: a ComponentInstance object if successful

methods.name: add_line
methods.summary: The add_line method is used to add an edge to the collection of entities.
methods.remarks: The add_line method is used to add an edge to the collection of entities. This is not to be confused with the concept of a “line” from a geometric sense, which is an invisible object represented by an Array of a point and a vector. (See the Array class for more information on geometric lines in SketchUp.) This method is the same as add_edges method, but returns a single edge.

params.name: point1
params.doc: Point3d object representing the edge's starting point.

params.name: point2
params.doc: Point3d object representing the edge's ending point.

returns.doc: a Edge object if successful

methods.name: add_ngon
methods.summary: The add_ngon method is used to create a multi-sided polygon.
methods.remarks: The add_ngon method is used to create a multi-sided polygon.

params.name: center
params.doc: A Point3d object representing the center of the polygon.

params.name: normal
params.doc: A Vector3d object.

params.name: radius
params.doc: A radius.

params.name: numsides
params.doc: The number of sides for the polygon.

returns.doc: an array of Edges that make up the polygon if successful

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the current object.

params.name: observer
params.doc: An observer.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: add_section_plane
methods.signature: add_section_plane(point, vector) => Sketchup::SectionPlane? | add_section_plane(plane) => Sketchup::SectionPlane? | add_section_plane(plane) => Sketchup::SectionPlane?
methods.summary: Adds a section plane object to the entities.
methods.remarks: Adds a section plane object to the entities. Refer to the Geom module for information on how planes are represented.

methods.name: add_snap
methods.signature: add_snap(position, direction) => Sketchup::Snap | add_snap(position, direction, up) => Sketchup::Snap
methods.summary: The #add_snap method is used to create a new Snap.
methods.remarks: The #add_snap method is used to create a new Snap.

methods.name: add_text
methods.signature: add_text(text, point, vector) => Sketchup::Text | add_text(text, instance_path_and_pt, vector) => Sketchup::Text | add_text(text, instance_array_and_pt, vector) => Sketchup::Text
methods.summary: The #add_text method adds a note or label text entity to the entities.
methods.remarks: The #add_text method adds a note or label text entity to the entities.

params.name: text
params.doc: The text string to add.

params.name: point
params.doc: A Point3d object representing the insertion point.

params.name: vector
params.doc: The Vector representing an arrow leader.

returns.doc: a Text object if successful

methods.name: at
methods.summary: The #at method is an alias for #[].
methods.remarks: The #at method is an alias for #[].

params.name: entity_index
params.doc: The index for a specific entity.

methods.name: build
methods.summary: Note: While using #build it is important to not add or remove vertices by other means than the builder.
methods.remarks: Note: While using #build it is important to not add or remove vertices by other means than the builder. Also don't modify the position of the vertices in the Sketchup::Entities collection. Doing so can break the vertex-cache that de-duplicates the vertices. Creates an Sketchup::EntitiesBuilder that can be used to generate bulk geometry with performance in mind. This is particularly useful for importers where the geometry is already well defined and one wants to recreate it without further processing. The call to #build starts an implicit operation, even if no other model changes are made within the block. This is not the same as Model#start_operation, so it's still recommended to wrap all model changes, including #build with Model#start_operation and Model#commit_operation. Refer to the documentation of Sketchup::EntitiesBuilder for more details.

methods.name: clear!
methods.summary: The clear! method is used to remove all entities from the collection of entities.
methods.remarks: The clear! method is used to remove all entities from the collection of entities.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each.
methods.remarks: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each. This would change the size of the collection and cause elements to be skipped as the indices change. Instead copy the current collection to an array using to_a and then use each on the array, when removing content. The #each method is used to iterate through the entities in the collection of entities.

methods.name: erase_entities
methods.summary: Note: It's faster to use this method and erase in bulk than to iterate individual drawing elements calling Drawingelement#erase!.
methods.remarks: Note: It's faster to use this method and erase in bulk than to iterate individual drawing elements calling Drawingelement#erase!. The #erase_entities method is used to erase one or more entities from the model.

throws.when: if the given entities contains instances or definitions are used by Model#active_path.

methods.name: fill_from_mesh
methods.signature: fill_from_mesh(polygon_mesh, weld_vertices = true, smooth_flags = Geom::PolygonMesh::AUTO_SOFTEN|Geom::PolygonMesh::SMOOTH_SOFT_EDGES, f_material = nil, b_material = nil) => Boolean
methods.summary: The #fill_from_mesh method is used to add faces and edges to the collection of entities from a Geom::PolygonMesh.
methods.remarks: The #fill_from_mesh method is used to add faces and edges to the collection of entities from a Geom::PolygonMesh. It requires that the entities collection to be filled is empty. It has higher performance than #add_faces_from_mesh, but does less error checking as it builds the geometry. The smooth_flags parameter can contain any of the following values if passed. The constants were added in SketchUp 2014. For previous versions, numeric values have to be specified instead of the Ruby constants: 0: Geom::PolygonMesh::NO_SMOOTH_OR_HIDE 1: Geom::PolygonMesh::HIDE_BASED_ON_INDEX (Negative point index will hide the edge.) 2: Geom::PolygonMesh::SOFTEN_BASED_ON_INDEX (Negative point index will soften the edge.) 4: Geom::PolygonMesh::AUTO_SOFTEN (Interior edges are softened.) 8: Geom::PolygonMesh::SMOOTH_SOFT_EDGES (All soft edges will also be smooth.) The 4rd and 5th parameters will accept a Material object or a string name of a material currently in the model.

params.name: weld_vertices
params.doc: This argument has no effect and is kept for compatibility reasons. Points are always merged.

params.name: smooth_flags
params.doc: flags for softening and smoothing of edges.

params.name: f_material
params.doc: material to paint front faces with.

params.name: b_material
params.doc: material to paint back faces with.

methods.name: intersect_with
methods.signature: intersect_with(recurse, transform1, entities1, transform2, hidden, entities2) => Array<Sketchup::Edge>
methods.summary: The #intersect_with method is used to intersect a Sketchup::Entities, Sketchup::Component, or Sketchup::Group object with a entities object.
methods.remarks: The #intersect_with method is used to intersect a Sketchup::Entities, Sketchup::Component, or Sketchup::Group object with a entities object. empty if no intersection(s) were found.

params.name: recurse
params.doc: true if you want this entities object to be recursed (intersection lines will be put inside of groups and components within this entities object).

params.name: transform1
params.doc: The transformation for this entities object.

params.name: entities1
params.doc: The entities object where you want the intersection lines to appear.

params.name: transform2
params.doc: The transformation for entities1.

params.name: hidden
params.doc: true if you want hidden geometry in this entities object to be used in the intersection.

params.name: entities2
params.doc: A single entity, or an array of entities.

returns.doc: The intersecting edges created. This array may be

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is used to retrieve the number of entities in the collection of entities.
methods.remarks: The #length method is used to retrieve the number of entities in the collection of entities.

methods.name: model
methods.summary: The model method is used to retrieve the model that contains the collection of entities.
methods.remarks: The model method is used to retrieve the model that contains the collection of entities.

returns.doc: the model that contains the collection of entities if successful.

methods.name: parent
methods.summary: The parent method is used to retrieve the parent or object that contains the collection of entities.
methods.remarks: The parent method is used to retrieve the parent or object that contains the collection of entities. A parent can be either a Model or ComponentDefinition object.

returns.doc: the object that contains the collection of entities if successful

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the current object.

params.name: observer
params.doc: An observer.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is an alias for the #length method.
methods.remarks: The #size method is an alias for the #length method.

methods.name: transform_by_vectors
methods.summary: The transform_by_vectors method is used to apply several vectors to several sub-entities all at once.
methods.remarks: The transform_by_vectors method is used to apply several vectors to several sub-entities all at once.

params.name: sub_entities
params.doc: An array of entities to transform.

params.name: vectors
params.doc: An array of vectors to apply.

methods.name: transform_entities
methods.summary: The transform_entities method is used to apply a transform to several sub-entities all at once.
methods.remarks: The transform_entities method is used to apply a transform to several sub-entities all at once. If you are transforming entities in the active drawing context or any of its parent drawing contexts, the transformation will be interpreted as relative to the global coordinate system. Otherwise the transformation will be interpreted as being on the local coordinate system.

params.name: transform
params.doc: The Transformation to apply.

params.name: entities
params.doc: An array or series of entities to transform.

returns.doc: false if the entities array was empty.

methods.name: weld
methods.summary: The #weld method takes a set of edges and find all possible chains of edges and connect them with a Curve.
methods.remarks: The #weld method takes a set of edges and find all possible chains of edges and connect them with a Curve. A curve will not cross another curve. They will split where multiple curves meet.

throws.when: if the given entities are not part of the same Sketchup::Entities collection.

properties.name: active_section_plane
properties.summary: The active_section_plane method is used to access the currently active section plane in the Entities object.
properties.remarks: The active_section_plane method is used to access the currently active section plane in the Entities object.
